name: Sierra de la Filera Peaks Loop
name_original: Peña Blanca, Pico Filera, Peña Negra y Peña Castillo – circular desde Abelgas de Luna por el Parque Natural Babia y Luna
description: Hike a difficult 10.1-mile (16.2 km) loop from Abelgas de Luna, gaining 2050 feet (625 metres) through Babia y Luna Natural Park.
region_intro: Hiking around Sena De Luna, nestled in the southern Cantabrian Mountains, features diverse landscapes including significant mountain passes and river valleys. The region is characterized by rugged terrain, with notable gorges like Los Calderones and the expansive Luna Reservoir. Hikers can explore a blend of mountainous areas, fertile meadows, and historical drover's roads. Best hiking trails around Sena De Luna The most popular hiking route is Las Tres Marías Trail, a 7.0 miles (11.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 30 minutes to complete. This route offers forest and mountain views within the Reserva de la Biosfera del Alto Bernesga. Another top favourite among local hikers is El Cordel de Merinas Loop from Abelgas de Luna, a moderate 5.7 miles (9.1 km) path. This trail explores historic drover's roads and scenic gorges. Local hikers also love the Cuitu Negru Loop Trail, a 9.8 miles (15.7 km) trail leading through the rugged Cantabrian Mountains, often completed in about 5 hours 20 minutes. Hiking around Sena De Luna is defined by the Cantabrian Mountains, deep gorges, and river valleys, with trails ranging from easy walks to challenging mountain ascents. The network offers options for different ability levels, including over 60 routes. The routes in Sena De Luna are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Sena De Luna's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Sena De Luna?
A: Sena De Luna offers a wide variety of hiking opportunities, with over 60 routes available. These trails cater to different skill levels, ranging from easy walks to more challenging mountain ascents within the Cantabrian Mountains.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Sena De Luna?
A: The terrain in Sena De Luna is diverse, featuring rugged mountain ranges, significant passes like La Cubilla Pass, and deep gorges such as Los Calderones. You'll also find fertile river valleys with meadows and arboreal areas, offering a mix of forest and mountain views. Trails often include historical drover's roads.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Sena De Luna has several easy hiking options. For instance, the Sendero Sena de Luna - Pobladura is a relatively easy circular route connecting the villages of Sena de Luna and Pobladura, perfect for families. Overall, there are 5 easy routes available in the area.

Q: Can I find challenging hikes in the Sena De Luna area?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a challenge, Sena De Luna offers 28 difficult routes. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1239840155/cuitu-negru-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuitu Negru Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 9.8 miles (15.7 km) long and leads through the rugged Cantabrian Mountains, typically taking over 5 hours to complete.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Sena De Luna?
A: Yes, there are several circular hiking routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1051376209/el-cordel-de-merinas-loop-from-abelgas-de-luna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cordel de Merinas Loop from Abelgas de Luna&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.7-mile (9.1 km) path exploring historic drover's roads and scenic gorges. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1051369702/sierra-de-la-filera-peaks-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de la Filera Peaks Loop&lt;/a&gt;, offering more strenuous views.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking?
A: Sena De Luna is rich in natural features and landmarks. You can explore the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4073598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Calderones Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, known for its geological and scenic value. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6333018" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cubilla Pass&lt;/a&gt; offers spectacular views, and the Luna Reservoir reveals a 'ghost town' when water levels are low. You might also encounter the impressive cliffs and crags of La Serrona.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Sena De Luna?
A: The best time to visit Sena De Luna for outdoor activities is typically during spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October). During these months, the weather is mild and pleasant, and the landscapes are particularly vibrant, making for ideal hiking conditions.

Q: Are there historical sites or cultural points of interest along the trails?
A: Yes, the region has a rich historical and cultural context. You can visit the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5558242" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of Manadero&lt;/a&gt;, a rock sanctuary inside the Cueva de las Palomas. Ancient drover's roads, like the Cordel de Merinas, also add a historical dimension to your hike. The 15th-century Ermita de Nuestra Señora de Pruneda in Rabanal de Luna is another cultural landmark.

Q: What do other hikers say about their experience in Sena De Luna?
A: The hiking routes in Sena De Luna are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from rugged mountains to serene river valleys, and the variety of trails suitable for different abilities.

Q: Is Sena De Luna a good destination for hiking with dogs?
A: While specific dog-friendly regulations can vary by trail or protected area, the natural and varied landscapes of Sena De Luna generally offer many opportunities for hiking with dogs. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock, and to check local signage for any restrictions.

Q: What are the parking options for trailheads in Sena De Luna?
A: Parking availability for trailheads in Sena De Luna typically varies by location. Many villages and popular starting points for trails will have designated parking areas, though these might be limited in smaller hamlets. It's advisable to check specific route details on komoot or local information boards for the most accurate parking advice for your chosen trail.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trails in Sena De Luna?
A: Public transport options to reach specific trailheads in Sena De Luna can be limited, as is common in many rural mountain regions. While there might be bus services connecting larger towns, accessing more remote starting points often requires personal transport. Planning ahead and checking local bus schedules is recommended if relying on public transport.
tips: Picturesque forest track on the path from Abelgas to Mallo that runs near the Cuartero stream.
Beautiful views of the imposing summit of La Viesca from the hill of the same name. A beautiful print.
A picturesque path with magnificent views that we find in the Collado del Remansadero, which separates the valleys that form the Arroyo de Campohermoso and the Arroyo del Reguerón.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Peña Blanca, Pico Filera, Peña Negra y Peña Castillo circular trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as difficult on komoot, covering over 16 kilometers with an elevation gain of approximately 625 meters. While some sections are described as uncomplicated under good visibility, the descent from Peña Negra can be challenging due to extensive and high vegetation, potentially requiring some off-trail navigation.

faqs.question: What is the estimated duration for completing the circular hike from Abelgas de Luna?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for completing this circular hike is around 5 hours and 6 minutes, though this can vary based on your pace, breaks, and weather conditions.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the hike from Abelgas de Luna?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from the picturesque village of Abelgas de Luna. You should be able to find parking within the village itself, which serves as the primary access point for this route.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features diverse terrain, starting from Abelgas de Luna at around 1300 meters and ascending into the Sierra de la Filera mountain range. You'll traverse a ridge connecting Peña Blanca and Pico Filera, experiencing a progressive climb. The landscape includes rough limestone mountain ridges, open high pastures, and potentially dense vegetation, especially on descents.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest and views along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter several notable points. These include the Path from Abelgas to Mallo, near the Cuartero stream, Collado La Viesca, and Collado del Remansadero. From Pico Filera, the highest point at 1873 meters, you'll get exceptional panoramic views of the Cantabrian Mountains, the valleys around Abelgas de Luna, and often the Luna River reservoir in the distance.

faqs.question: What kind of flora and fauna might I encounter in the Babia y Luna Natural Park?
faqs.answer: The Babia y Luna Natural Park is a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve known for its rich biodiversity. You might see lush vegetation including oaks, gall oaks, beeches, birches, and numerous endemic plant species. The park is also home to diverse wildlife, such as the Cantabrian brown bear, the elusive Iberian desman, and various birds like the Egyptian vulture and peregrine falcon.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike the Peña Blanca, Pico Filera, Peña Negra y Peña Castillo route?
faqs.answer: Given the 'high mountain landscape' feel and potential for challenging conditions, the best time to hike this route is typically during the warmer, drier months, from late spring through early autumn. This avoids snow and ice, and ensures better visibility and more manageable terrain.

faqs.question: Can I bring my dog on this hike?
faqs.answer: While the Babia y Luna Natural Park generally welcomes visitors, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and livestock, especially in a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. Be prepared for varied terrain and potential encounters with dense vegetation.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for hiking in Babia y Luna Natural Park?
faqs.answer: As of current information, there are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required for day hiking on established trails within the Babia y Luna Natural Park. However, it's always a good practice to check with local tourism offices or park authorities for any updated regulations before your visit.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: For this challenging hike, you should bring appropriate hiking boots, layers of clothing suitable for mountain weather, plenty of water, snacks, a map or GPS device (like komoot's app) for navigation, and sun protection. Trekking poles can be very helpful, especially for the descents through potentially dense vegetation.

faqs.question: Is this a circular route, or an out-and-back trail?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route, meaning you will start and end in Abelgas de Luna, completing a full loop through the various peaks and passes mentioned in the trail name.

waypoints.name: Path from Abelgas to Mallo, near the Cuartero stream
